evidence.NEVER_SIGNED_IN: Active, licensed account with no sign-in record.
evidence.EXT_PRIVILEGED: External account with a privileged role: {roles}.
evidence.DISABLED_WITH_ASSETS: Disabled account still holds roles and/or licenses.
evidence.LICENSE_WASTE: License assigned, account inactive for {days} days.
evidence.NO_MANAGER_PRIVILEGED: Privileged account with no assigned manager.
evidence.SYNC_ANOMALY: Privileged account synced from on-prem (cloud-only recommended).
evidence.UNUSED_PRIVILEGE.no_signins: Roles {roles}: no sign-ins in the last {window} days.
evidence.UNUSED_PRIVILEGE.inactive: Roles {roles}: last sign-in {days} days ago — privileges unused.
evidence.NIGHT_SIGNINS.priv: {count} sign-ins of a PRIVILEGED account between 20:00–04:00 (UTC).
evidence.STALE_PASSWORD: Password unchanged for {days} days and {mfa_note} — the password is the only/primary protection.
evidence.MANY_FAILED_SIGNINS: {count} failed sign-ins in the last {window} days — possible password spray / brute force.{note}
evidence.MANY_FAILED_SIGNINS.note_legacy: Legacy auth attempts seen in parallel — a typical password-spray signature.
evidence.MULTIPLE_PRIV_ROLES: {count} privileged roles on a single account: {roles}.
evidence.NEW_PRIVILEGED_ACCOUNT: Privileged account created {days} days ago — verify its origin.
evidence.EXTERNAL_MEMBER: Identity outside the verified domains appears as 'member' (not guest).
evidence.PRIV_COMPROMISE_SIGNALS.verify: Verify: Entra ID → Monitoring → Sign-in logs, filter on this user (columns: Risk state, Client app, Status).
evidence.RISKY_USER_UNREMEDIATED.base: Risk state '{state}' (level {level}, updated {when}{detail}) is sitting unhandled in Identity Protection.{note} Verify: Entra ID → Identity Protection → Risky users.
evidence.RISKY_USER_UNREMEDIATED.note_pr: The account has a privileged role!
evidence.CA_MFA_EXCLUDED.note_priv: The account has a privileged role — excluding an admin from MFA is an open door.
evidence.SHADOW_PRIVILEGE: Privileged role inherited solely through membership: {chains}.{extra}
evidence.SHADOW_PRIVILEGE.extra: On top of that the account is weakly protected: {weak}.
attack.risky: {count} sign-ins flagged risky by Identity Protection in a {window}-day window
attack.failed_soft: {count} failed sign-ins in {window} days (could be an attack, but just as likely an expired password in an old client)
attack.risky_user: account flagged '{state}' by Identity Protection (level {level}, state from {when}) — alert UNHANDLED
evidence.APP_SECRET_EXPIRED: {count} expired credential(s) ({kinds}) on an enabled application.
evidence.APP_SECRET_EXPIRING: Credential expires in {days} days (threshold {warn}) — plan the rotation.
evidence.APP_LONG_LIVED_SECRET: Credential valid for {days} days (>{limit}) — a long leak window.
evidence.APP_SECRET_OVER_CERT: Application authenticates with a client secret, with no certificate (prefer a certificate).
evidence.APP_NO_OWNER: Application without an owner — no attestation or business accountability.
evidence.APP_UNVERIFIED_PRIVILEGED: Application with a high-risk permission has no verified publisher (MPN).
evidence.APP_CREDENTIAL_SPRAWL: {count} credentials ({secrets} secret(s), {certs} cert(s)) — sprawl, harder to rotate and control.
evidence.APP_WIDE_CONSENT: {count} users with individual consent — broad exposure ('illicit consent' vector).
evidence.APP_ORPHANED: No owner and no assigned users, yet the application has a credential — likely abandoned.
evidence.APP_DORMANT_PRIVILEGED.inactive: Service principal last signed in {days} days ago (threshold {dormant}), while the application holds app-only permissions: {perms}. Unused high-risk permissions — revoke or disable the application.
evidence.APP_DORMANT_PRIVILEGED.never: No service-principal sign-in at all in the activity data, while the application holds app-only permissions: {perms}. A dead payload to revoke.
evidence.APP_CREDENTIAL_ADDED: Credential changes in the last {window} days: {items}.{risk}
evidence.APP_CREDENTIAL_ADDED.risk_note: The application has high-risk app-only permissions — confirm the change was authorized.
evidence.APP_PRIV_OWNER_WEAK.base: Weakly protected owners: {hits} — an owner can add their own secret and take over the app-only permissions ({perms}).
evidence.APP_PRIV_OWNER_WEAK.audit: NOTE: audit logs a credential change on {date} by {actor} — confirm it was authorized.
evidence.APP_GUEST_REACH: Guest accounts have access to the application — {parts}.
evidence.GROUP_ROLE_ASSIGNABLE_PRIV: Group grants privileged roles: {roles}. {who} inherit this access.
evidence.GROUP_ROLE_ASSIGNABLE.flagged: Group marked role-assignable (may receive directory roles).
evidence.GROUP_OWNERLESS: No owner — the group is neither attested nor managed.
evidence.GROUP_PUBLIC_M365: Public Microsoft 365 group — anyone in the org can join and read its content.
evidence.GROUP_ONPREM_ROLE: On-prem group grants cloud roles: {roles} (on-prem → Entra escalation).
evidence.GROUP_LICENSE_NO_MEMBERS: Group assigns licenses ({licenses}) but has no members.
evidence.GROUP_EMPTY: Group is empty (0 members) — a deletion candidate (directory hygiene).
evidence.GROUP_LARGE_PRIVILEGED: Group grants privileged roles ({roles}) and has {count} members — a large blast radius.
evidence.GROUP_DYNAMIC_PRIVILEGED: A dynamic rule automatically grants {what} — an account attribute decides access.
evidence.GROUP_NESTED: Group contains {count} nested group(s): {names} — nesting complicates the access audit.
evidence.GROUP_PRIV_WEAK_MEMBERS: {count} weakly protected members inherit a privileged role: {shown}
posture.no_mfa_policy: No enabled Conditional Access policy requires MFA, and security defaults are off — the tenant enforces MFA on nobody. The MFA registrations shown in the Accounts module are then merely a declaration.
posture.legacy_block: No enabled Conditional Access policy blocks legacy authentication.{note} Add a 'block' policy for clientAppTypes exchangeActiveSync/other.
posture.legacy_block.note_success: The logs show SUCCESSFUL legacy sign-ins — this is the direct cause.
posture.report_only: {count} CA policies in report-only mode ({names}{more}) — they report but enforce NOTHING. Verify whether this is a deliberate pilot stage.
posture.mfa_excluded: {count} accounts excluded from policies that require MFA — details in the account cards (rule CA_MFA_EXCLUDED). Exclusions can be legitimate (break-glass), but they must be countable and monitored.
posture.users_consent: Users can grant consent to applications themselves — the classic 'illicit consent grant' vector. Consider an admin consent workflow.
posture.users_register: Users can register their own applications in the tenant — every registration is a potential new entry point. Consider restricting it to designated roles.
posture.guest_member_level: Guests have member-level directory access (guestUserRoleId = member-level) — they see the full directory. Recommended level: 'restricted'.
posture.weak_methods: Weak authentication methods enabled: {methods} — vulnerable to SIM-swap / phishing. Prefer Authenticator / FIDO2 and phase out SMS/Voice.
posture.risky_users: {count} accounts with UNHANDLED risk in Identity Protection (atRisk/confirmedCompromised) — alerts sitting without a response.
escalation.identity.title: {upn} — unprotected admin
escalation.group.title: {upn} → {group}
escalation.app.title: {upn} → {app}
insight.priv_no_mfa: {count} privileged accounts WITHOUT MFA — a direct takeover target.
insight.priv_groups: {count} groups grant privileged roles — access via membership, often off the role-review radar.
insight.high_risk_apps: {count} applications have high-risk app-only permissions — they work without a user and bypass MFA.
insight.expired_apps: {count} applications with an expired credential on an enabled account — dead code or a broken integration to clean up.
insight.license_blast: Group licensing covers ~{count} assignments — a change in one group spreads widely.
insight.legacy_success: {count} accounts with SUCCESSFUL legacy auth — a real MFA bypass; the source needs urgent verification.
insight.legacy_attempts: {count} accounts with legacy-auth attempts — all blocked (Conditional Access works); verify the protocol list.
legacy.fallback: Legacy client — verify the source in the sign-in log.
legacy.Authenticated SMTP: Sending via SMTP AUTH — typically printers/scanners/apps. Often deliberately tolerated.
legacy.Exchange ActiveSync: Native mobile mail clients (EAS). Usually to migrate to Outlook mobile (modern auth).
legacy.Exchange Web Services: EWS — older integrations/CRM. Migrate to the Graph API.
legacy.Exchange Online PowerShell: Remote EXO PowerShell — module V1. Move to module V2/V3 (modern auth).
legacy.IMAP4: IMAP mail retrieval — third-party clients. Disable if unused.
legacy.POP3: POP mail retrieval — obsolete. Disable if unused.
legacy.MAPI Over HTTP: Older Outlook transport. Usually to be disabled.
legacy.Offline Address Book: OAB download by an old client.
legacy.Outlook Anywhere (RPC over HTTP): Old Outlook transport (RPC/HTTP). Migrate to MAPI/HTTP + modern auth.
legacy.Reporting Web Services: EXO reporting over an old protocol.
legacy.Other clients: Unrecognized legacy client — needs manual verification in the log.

cli.write.denied: {label}: no access to {name} (file open/locked?). Saving as {alt}.
cli.write.denied_final: {label}: still no access — close any open reports or point to a different --out.
cli.outdir.denied: No access to the output directory: {dir}

Run the scanner first (mode [1]) or point to a folder: [white]build --dir <path>[/]

theatrics.title: AccessGuy report engine
theatrics.stage.score: scoring objects against the rule set
theatrics.stage.posture: evaluating tenant posture · Conditional Access + policies
tmpl.brand.sub: Periodic Microsoft Entra ID access review
tmpl.module.brand_sub: Microsoft Entra ID access review · module
tmpl.footer.disclaimer: The grade is a supporting heuristic, not a compliance standard. Methodology: contracts/rules.yaml.
tmpl.footer.methodology: Methodology: the scoring rubric in contracts/rules.yaml.
tmpl.footer.methodology_groups: Methodology: the scoring rubric in contracts/rules.yaml (groupRules section).
tmpl.footer.methodology_apps: Methodology: the scoring rubric in contracts/rules.yaml (appRules section).
tmpl.fact.name: Name
tmpl.sum.no_insights: No material risk insights — very good hygiene.
tmpl.sum.posture_h2: Tenant configuration — why these risks exist at all
tmpl.sum.posture_intro: Conditional Access and tenant policies (schema 1.3). A configuration gap explains the symptoms seen in the modules — e.g. successful legacy auth comes from a missing block policy.
tmpl.sum.posture_clean: Configuration is clean — MFA enforced, legacy blocked, consent under control.
tmpl.sum.esc_h2: Escalation paths — concrete chains to take over the tenant
tmpl.sum.esc_intro: Correlation of identities, groups and applications with sign-in logs. Each chain is a real route from a weakly protected account to privileged permissions — with evidence from the logs.
tmpl.sum.plan_intro: All findings from the three modules aggregated into a task list. Order = priority (risk weight × scale in this tenant). The examples link to specific cards in the detailed reports.
tmpl.sum.headline: Main signal:
tmpl.ca.findings_none: No posture findings — configuration looks solid.
tmpl.ca.not_collected: Conditional Access / tenant posture was not collected in this scan (needs Policy.Read.All).
tmpl.ca.excluded_note: Accounts excluded from MFA-enforcing policies (weakly covered — verify each is intentional):
tmpl.ca.excluded_none: No accounts are excluded from MFA policies.
tmpl.users.lic_none: No license data (collector skipped or no permissions).
tmpl.users.ga_none: No accounts with the Global Administrator role detected.
tmpl.users.nomfa_all_ok: All accounts have registered MFA (or no data).
tmpl.users.legacy_h3_note: (successful = real MFA bypass; blocked = CA works)
tmpl.users.legacy_hint: Hint: a protocol with the 'Successful' column = 0 means the attempts were blocked (e.g. by Conditional Access) — they appear in the log but are not a takeover. Focus on rows with 'Successful' > 0.
tmpl.users.legacy_none: No legacy-auth sign-ins in the observation window (or no signIns data).
tmpl.users.flags_none: No red flags — account is clean.
tmpl.groups.priv_none: No groups with a privileged role.
tmpl.groups.flags_none: No red flags — group is clean.
tmpl.groups.empty: No groups in the dataset (the 'groups' collector did not run or the tenant has none).
tmpl.apps.high_risk_none: No application has high-risk app-only permissions.
tmpl.apps.creds_none: No credentials (secret/certificate) — the app may use federation or be inactive.
tmpl.apps.group_members_none: This group's member list was not fetched (outside the group scan scope/limit).
tmpl.apps.flags_none: No red flags — application is clean.
tmpl.apps.empty: No applications in the dataset (the 'apps' collector did not run or the tenant has none).
branding.caption: AccessGuy · Read-only Microsoft Entra ID access review · Daniel „NewLife" Budyn
posture.mfa_narrow: Conditional Access enforces MFA only through narrowly scoped policies ({count}) — none targets all users and all applications, so most sign-ins may go unprotected. Add a broad 'require MFA for All users / All cloud apps' policy (with documented break-glass exclusions).
